RECORD TABLE

The third column specifies which sections or sub -sections have been updated.  The fourth  
column should mention the version of the product to which the change applies.

I INTRODUCTION  
 
I.1 Product name 
 
Product name Dataset name 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_BALTIC_OceanColour_trend omi_health_chl_baltic_oceanclour_trend 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_BALTIC_OceanColour_area_averaged_mean omi_health_chl_baltic_oceanclour_area_averaged_mean 
OMI_HEALTH_CHL_BALTIC_OceanColour_area_averaged_anomaly N/A 
 
I.2 Indicator evaluation method 
 
The CMEMS Ocean Colour OMIs are derived from the reprocessed (MY) regional gridded  
chlorophyll products generated and distributed by the CMEMS OC -TAC. The input dataset are 
the spectral remote sensing reflectances (Rrs) produced by the Plymouth Marine Laboratory  
(PML) using an ad -hoc configuration for CMEMS of the ESA OC -CCI processor version 6 (OC - 
CCIv6) to merge at 1km resolution (rather than at 4km as for OC -CCI) MERIS, MODIS -AQUA, 
SeaWiFS, NPP -VIIRS and OLCI -A data. The chlorophyll product is derived from a Multi -Layer 
Perceptron neural-net (MLP) developed on field measurements collected within the BiOMaP  
program of JRC/EC (Zibordi et al., 2011). The algorithm  is an ensemble of different MLPs that  
use Rrs at different wavelengths as input. The processing chain and the techniques used to  
develop the algorithm are detailed in Brando et al. (2021; 2024). 
The MY regional chlorophyll dataset was used to compute anomaly map, with respect to  
reference climatology (1997-2014), daily and monthly regional mean time series and trend map. 
Trend map was obtained by applying Colella et al. (2016) methodology. Monthly chlorophyll  
maps, derived from MY data, are used to com pute the trend for the period 1997 -2023. Trend 
analysis has been performed by using the X -11 seasonal adjustment procedure (Pezzulli et al.  
2005). The X -11 procedure assumes that the CHL time series Y t can be decomposed into its  
three components: the seaso nal signal S t, the inter -annual component T t, and an irregular  
component It: 
Yt = Tt + St + It 
The deseasonalized time series is obtained through an iterative process (Colella et al., 2016), by 
using successive filters, from simple moving-average, seasonal moving-average and Henderson 
filters (Vantrepotte and Mélin, 2009). At the end of this pr

catalog_entry.summary: This document is a Quality Information Document for Ocean Health Chl BALTIC OceanColour, detailing the evaluation method and product information of the CMEMS Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI). It provides details on contributors, approval dates, and changes in quality over time.
